nonGoals: Do not create the test file
Do not wire into any dispatch/HTTP pipeline
Do not modify chunkText, normalizeText, or wordCount
Do not add provider-specific maxLength lookups
objective: Create prepareForSynthesis composition module that imports and composes normalizeText + chunkText + wordCount
planText: Create apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.ts. Import normalizeText and wordCount from the text-utilities barrel at '../lib/text-utilities'. Import chunkText from './chunk-text'. Export the SynthesisChunk interface ({ text: string; wordCount: number }). Export the prepareForSynthesis(text: string, maxLength: number): SynthesisChunk[] function with this flow: normalize input via normalizeText → pass normalized text to chunkText(normalized, maxLength) → map each chunk to { text: chunk, wordCount: wordCount(chunk) } → return the array. Empty/whitespace input naturally returns [] because chunkText returns [] for empty strings. Non-positive maxLength is delegated to chunkText's existing throw. Verify with npm run build:web for type checking and compilation, and npm run test to confirm existing tests still pass.

nonGoals: Do not modify the prepareForSynthesis module unless a bug is found that must be fixed
Do not add integration or E2E tests
Do not test the underlying normalizeText/chunkText/wordCount utilities directly
objective: Create vitest suite for prepareForSynthesis covering all acceptance criteria from issue #50
planText: Create apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.test.ts as a vitest suite (import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'). Import prepareForSynthesis from './prepare-for-synthesis'. Cover six acceptance criteria: (1) empty/whitespace returns [] — test empty string, spaces-only, tabs/newlines; (2) single chunk under limit returns one SynthesisChunk with correct wordCount; (3) multi-chunk splitting returns multiple elements and chunks.join('') reconstructs the normalized input; (4) each chunk's wordCount field matches the actual word count of chunk.text; (5) invariant that every chunk.text.length <= maxLength; (6) non-positive maxLength (0, -1, -Infinity) throws. Use representative English prose as test input. Run npm run test to verify all new tests pass alongside existing suite.
